content.text:
{For from him and through him and to him are all things.}{Romans 11:36}
 {The purpose of computing is insight, not numbers.}{— Richard Hamming}

The **rank** of a matrix captures its "size" in terms of 
linear algebra—how much of the codomain it can reach.

content.title: Rank and Nullity
content.content:
For an $m \times n$ matrix $A$:

- The **rank** of $A$ is $\rank(A) = \dim(\Col(A))$, 
 the dimension of the column space.

- The **nullity** of $A$ is 
 $\nullity(A) = \dim(\Null(A))$, the dimension of the null space.

content.title: Rank Equals Number of Pivots
content.content: The rank of $A$ equals the number of pivot columns in $A$.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Finding rank and nullity
content.solution:
Row reduce: $A \to \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 & 0 & -2 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 & 2 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \end{bmatrix}$.

There are 2 pivot columns, so $\rank(A) = 2$.

There are 2 free variables, so $\nullity(A) = 2$.

Note: $\rank(A) + \nullity(A) = 2 + 2 = 4 = n$ (number of columns).

content.text:
This theorem says: the dimension of the column space plus the 
dimension of the null space equals the number of columns.

content.title: The Invertible Matrix Theorem
content.content:
For an $n \times n$ matrix $A$, the following are equivalent:

1. $A$ is invertible.

2. $A\vect{x} = \vect{b}$ has a unique solution for every $\vect{b}$.

3. $A\vect{x} = \vect{0}$ has only the trivial solution.

4. The columns of $A$ are linearly independent.

5. The columns of $A$ span $\R^n$.

6. $\Col(A) = \R^n$.

7. $\rank(A) = n$.

8. $\Null(A) = \{\vect{0}\}$.

9. $\det(A) \neq 0$.

10. The RREF of $A$ is $I_n$.
content.label: Theorem

marginNotes.text:
The earth is the Lord's and the fullness 
thereof, the world and those who dwell therein.
